result:
`roadmap.md` is written. Here's what it gives you and the calls that drive it.

## The core argument
Your instinct was right — "sort by value, draw a line" breaks here for four specific reasons the columns hide:

**Two Med-rated items are secretly the most important on the board:**
- **BL-11 (audit-log pipeline)** is the foundation under BL-02. The SOC 2 evidence dashboard *cannot* be built on today's unstructured stdout logs — so the real audit deliverable is a 10-ew BL-11→BL-02 chain, and missing the **8 Sep freeze slips the audit a full quarter** and the deals waiting on it. It starts week 1.
- **BL-03 (identity service)** is the foundation under your sales-loud SAML SSO. BL-04 "resolves logins to a role and team membership" — nothing stores that today. So "SSO this quarter" actually costs **11 ew, not 5**. A value sort schedules BL-04 and assumes the foundation is free.

**Two important-looking items can't ship as written:**
- **BL-07 (live dashboards)** — the splashy demo prospects ask for by name — is built on **Streamflow, which dies 30 Sep**. Anything built on it this quarter is throwaway. Hard cut, with a recommendation to scope the streaming replacement separately.
- **BL-09 (EU residency)** as written runs EU data in **us-east** — a deal-losing compliance breach, and the data isn't even reachable there. Cut and re-scope; the honest eu-central version is bigger than 4 ew.

## The plan shape
- **~50 ew realistic capacity** (not the 78 headline), of which I **committed only ~40**, holding ~10 as a ranked stretch tier + buffer.
- **Committed:** BL-11, BL-03, BL-16 (early) → BL-02, BL-04, BL-08, BL-01 (mid) → BL-13, BL-15 (late).
- **Stretch, in order:** BL-25, BL-20, BL-19.
- **Designated release valve:** BL-15 (onboarding drip) slips to early Q4 if the haircut bites — the audit spine and SSO chain never flex.

## The opinionated cut to defend on Friday
I deferred **BL-23 (AI summaries)** — leadership's favorite — to Q4 *and recommend it as the Q4 headline*, because it has no deadline or dependency forcing it into Q3 while the audit and SSO both contractually gate deals. That's the one the skeptical room will push on hardest, so it's argued explicitly.

Hidden ordering chains flagged so they don't surprise you in week 8: BL-11→BL-02, BL-03→BL-04/BL-01/BL-19/BL-25, BL-13→BL-15, plus the EU-residency landmine that also affects BL-22.

Assumptions (capacity math, the four dependency inferences, BL-09's re-estimate, phase mapping) are tagged `[ASSUMPTION]` inline so you can challenge or correct any of them before the review.

One thing worth your steer: if engineering can give a firmer read on the **summer-PTO bite**, that's the number that decides how much of the stretch tier you can actually promise — want me to tighten the plan once you have it?
